steps.title: Find the subject
steps.content: The subject of the sentence is talents, a plural noun. The phrase of Barbara Chase-Riboud identifies whose talents they are, but it does not change the subject's number.

steps.title: Choose plural agreement
steps.content: A, B, and C are singular verb forms, so they do not agree with the plural subject talents. The sentence needs a plural present-tense form before limited. The answer is D.
